scripts.subtitle: Classic Stop / Real-talk opener
scripts.original_vo: Stop. Real talk for every business owner paying Chinese suppliers. You send $50,000 to your factory. Your supplier gets $48,000. Where did $2,000 go? Your bank's hidden exchange rate margin ate $1,200. Intermediary fees took the rest. And here's the kicker. Your supplier won't start production until they see the full amount. So now you're losing a week. Maybe two. While you're scrambling to wire the difference. Listen. WorldFirst sends the same $50,000 at near-interbank rate. That's the actual rate banks use with each other. Same day to Chinese bank accounts. No mystery fees. No supplier holding your order hostage. Stop letting your bank rob you on every payment. Search WorldFirst. Now.

scripts.subtitle: I'll say it opinion-as-truth + compound math
scripts.original_vo: I'll say it. Your bank is the most expensive part of your supply chain and nobody talks about it. You wire $50,000 to your Chinese supplier. They get $48,000. That's $2,000 gone. On one payment. Now run the math. Ten payments a year. That's $20,000. Fifty payments. That's $100,000. That's a hire. That's inventory. That's your margin. And here's what your bank won't tell you. That 2.5% exchange rate margin is hidden in the rate. You'll never see it on a statement. WorldFirst uses near-interbank rate. Same-day to Chinese accounts. The difference goes back into your business. Where it belongs. Period.

scripts.subtitle: Stop using your bank / right-tool reframe
scripts.original_vo: Stop using your bank to pay Chinese suppliers. I'm serious. Your bank is a tool. It's the wrong tool. You wouldn't use a hammer to cut wood. So why are you using a domestic bank to send international supplier payments? Here's what happens. You send $50,000. Hidden 2.5% margin. That's $1,200. Plus intermediary fees, another $800. Supplier gets $48,000. Refuses to start production. You lose a week. WorldFirst is built for this. Near-interbank rate. Same-day to Chinese bank accounts. Full $50,000 lands. Supplier starts that day. Use the right tool for the job. Your margin depends on it. Search WorldFirst. Now.

scripts.subtitle: Truth nobody's telling you / conspiratorial
scripts.original_vo: Here's the truth nobody's telling you about international wires. Your bank doesn't make money on the wire fee. They make money on the exchange rate. You send $50,000 to your Chinese supplier. The bank quotes you a rate that's 2.5% worse than the real one. That's $1,200 they pocket. Silently. Then intermediary banks take another $800 in fees that nobody warned you about. Supplier gets $48,000. Won't start production. You lose a week of lead time. This is how it works. Every single time. WorldFirst doesn't play this game. Near-interbank rate. Same-day to Chinese accounts. The rate you see is the rate you get. That's the difference. Period.

scripts.subtitle: Real talk + escalating personal energy
scripts.original_vo: Real talk for every business owner sourcing from China. You think your bank is helping you. They're not. You wire $50,000 to your supplier. They get $48,000. You call the bank. They blame the intermediary. The intermediary blames the receiving bank. Nobody takes responsibility. Meanwhile your supplier won't start production. You're losing days. Your customers are waiting. Your margin is bleeding. And you're on the phone with a teller who has no idea what you're talking about. I see this every week. Every single week. Stop. WorldFirst. Near-interbank rate. Same-day to Chinese accounts. Full $50,000 lands. Supplier starts. You sleep. Search WorldFirst. Now.
